21.correct: The First Schedule of the Constitution lists the names of the States and Union Territories and their territories.

wrong.B: The Second Schedule deals with salaries/emoluments of key officials.
wrong.C: The Third Schedule contains forms of oaths and affirmations.
wrong.D: The Fourth Schedule allocates Rajya Sabha seats to states/UTs.

21.fact: The First Schedule has been amended several times as new states were created.

22.correct: Part XVII of the Constitution deals with the Official Language.

wrong.B: Part XVI deals with special provisions for certain classes.
wrong.D: Part XVIII deals with emergency provisions.

22.fact: Articles 343-351 (Part XVII) cover the official languages of the Union and the states.

23.correct: Naati (Nati) is the traditional folk dance of Himachal Pradesh.

wrong.C: West Bengal has Gaudiya and other forms.
wrong.D: Rajasthan is known for Ghoomar and Kalbeliya.

23.fact: A mass Kullu Nati once entered the Guinness World Records as the largest folk dance.

24.correct: Mass remains the same on both the Earth and the Moon, as it is the amount of matter in an object.

wrong.A: Weight changes (it is less on the Moon).
wrong.C: Gravitational acceleration is different on the Moon.
wrong.D: Pressure can differ depending on conditions.

24.fact: Weight = mass x gravity; gravity on the Moon is about one-sixth of Earth's.

25.correct: William Wordsworth's collaborator in publishing 'Lyrical Ballads' (1798) was Samuel Taylor Coleridge.

wrong.A: Percy Bysshe Shelley was a later Romantic poet.
wrong.C: Lord Byron was another Romantic poet, not the collaborator.
wrong.D: Robert Southey was a contemporary but not the co-author.

25.fact: 'Lyrical Ballads' is considered the starting point of the English Romantic movement.

26.correct: If the Earth were twice its size, its larger shadow would make a lunar eclipse last longer.

wrong.B: The eclipse would not become shorter with a larger shadow.
wrong.C: There would be a change, not 'no change'.
wrong.D: The duration, not just the frequency, is what is mainly affected.

26.fact: A lunar eclipse occurs when the Earth's shadow falls on the Moon.

27.correct: The current Olympic flag (with five rings) was first flown at the 1920 Summer Olympics in Antwerp.

wrong.B: 1924 Summer Olympics is after its first flying.
wrong.C: The 1920 Winter Olympics did not exist (Winter Games began in 1924).
wrong.D: The 1924 Winter Olympics is not when it was first flown.

27.fact: The five interlocking rings represent the five inhabited continents.

28.correct: The strong, steady rise in India's foreign-exchange reserves over time shows that the 1991 reforms improved external-sector stability.

wrong.A: Reserves rose, they did not continuously fall, after 1991.
wrong.B: The aim was to reduce vulnerability, and reserves grew.
wrong.D: Foreign investment inflows increased, not declined.

28.fact: In 1991 India's reserves had fallen to just a few weeks of imports, prompting the reforms.

29.correct: Abscisic acid is the plant hormone that inhibits growth and can cause wilting (it also promotes dormancy and stomatal closure).
29.fact: Abscisic acid is often called the 'stress hormone' of plants.

30.correct: The 'four-quarter' format in international hockey was introduced in 2014.
30.fact: Field hockey switched from two 35-minute halves to four 15-minute quarters.

31.correct: A triathlon is contested in the sequence: swim, then cycle (bike), then run.

wrong.B: It does not start with running.
wrong.C: It does not start with cycling.
wrong.D: Running comes last, not before cycling.

31.fact: The triathlon became an Olympic sport at the Sydney 2000 Games.

32.correct: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) was established in 1982.

wrong.A: 1969 was the year of bank nationalization.

32.fact: NABARD is the apex development bank for agriculture and the rural economy.

33.correct: The 'Jeevan Pramaan' initiative relates to issuing digital life certificates for pensioners.

wrong.A: It is not about rural electrification.
wrong.B: It is not a skill-development scheme.
wrong.C: It is not a farmer-insurance scheme.

33.fact: Jeevan Pramaan uses biometric (Aadhaar) authentication so pensioners need not appear in person.

34.correct: In recent years, the People's Republic of China has been India's largest trading partner (by total trade).

wrong.A: The USA is among the top partners but China has often led in total trade.
wrong.C: The UAE is a major partner but not the largest overall.
wrong.D: Japan is a significant partner but not the largest.

34.fact: India runs a large trade deficit with China, importing far more than it exports.

35.correct: The DAJGUA (Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan) campaign, seen in news in July 2025, relates to tribal welfare.

wrong.B: It is not the Swachh Bharat campaign.
wrong.C: It is not an OBC scholarship scheme.
wrong.D: It is not a food-fortification programme.

35.fact: DAJGUA aims to saturate tribal villages with basic services and infrastructure.

36.correct: The Lichchhavi princess married to Chandragupta I was named Kumaradevi.

wrong.A: Kadambadevi is not the correct name.
wrong.D: Prabhavati was a Gupta princess married to a Vakataka king.

36.fact: The Gupta-Lichchhavi alliance strengthened Chandragupta I's position; coins commemorate this marriage.

37.correct: Section 290 of the BNSS, 2023 makes plea bargaining time-bound (e.g. application within 30 days of charges).
37.fact: Plea bargaining allows a negotiated settlement for certain less-serious offences.

38.correct: The Self-Respect Movement (Swabhiman/Self-Respect) was started by E.V. Ramaswamy Naicker (Periyar).

wrong.C: Sri Narayana Guru led reform in Kerala.
wrong.D: Jyotirao Phule led the Satyashodhak Samaj in Maharashtra.

38.fact: Periyar's movement opposed caste discrimination and Brahminical dominance in Tamil Nadu.

39.correct: As per the cited report, women constitute about 64.4% of India's agricultural workforce.
39.fact: Women play a major role in Indian agriculture, especially in sowing, weeding and harvesting.

40.correct: Anna University was ranked around 111 in the Times Higher Education (THE) Asia University Rankings 2025.
40.fact: Anna University, in Chennai, is a leading technical university in India.
